Dear Sir or Madam,

I am writing to request an immediate explanation and a refund regarding a charge of over 100 PLN that was taken from my bank account in connection with your service.

I signed up solely to complete a test and gain one-time access to the results. At no point during the payment process was I clearly and explicitly informed that I was entering into a paid subscription or that my card would be charged on a recurring basis.

3. Written confirmation that the subscription has been canceled.

If this matter is not resolved promptly, I will proceed with a chargeback through my bank and report the issue to the relevant consumer protection authorities.

I look forward to your prompt response.

Yours sincerely,
Zuzanna Wal

json.Title: Legal Notice of Fraudulent Activities
json.Description:
----------------------------------------------

Sonali Fabiani, Jan 13, 2026, 21:15

To Whom It May Concern,

I am writing to formally notify you of an unauthorized and deceptive billing practice carried out by your company, Personality Co.

On 12/11/2025, I consented only to a one-time payment of £1.95. At no point did I knowingly or explicitly consent to a recurring monthly subscription of £29.95, nor was such a subscription clearly disclosed at the time of payment.

Despite this, my card has been charged £29.95 on a recurring basis. Furthermore, there is no functional or accessible method to cancel or unsubscribe, which constitutes a serious consumer rights violation.

This practice appears to be:

- Misrepresentation of services

- Lack of informed consent

- Failure to provide a clear cancellation mechanism

- An unfair and potentially unlawful commercial practice

I hereby demand the following within 7 days of receipt of this email:

1. Immediate cancellation of any and all subscriptions associated with my account

2. A full refund of all unauthorized charges (£29.95 per month/sometimes more than once a month charged to date)

3. Written confirmation that no further charges will be made

If this matter is not resolved promptly, I will have no choice but to:

- Initiate a chargeback with my bank for unauthorized transactions

- File formal complaints with relevant consumer protection and regulatory authorities (including UK Trading Standards and the FCA, where applicable)

- Seek legal advice regarding recovery of funds and damages

Please treat this matter with urgency. This email serves as formal notice of dispute.

I expect written confirmation of resolution without delay.

Sincerely,

Sonali Fabiani

Subscriber email: sonalifabiani@gmail.com

Card ending in 9951

Lauren Higham, Jan 13, 2026, 23:59

Dear Sir or Madam,

I am writing to formally complain and request a refund regarding unexpected subscription charges taken from my bank account. I am emailing on behalf of myself and my child- my child's email address is charly.cawley@hotmail.com which is the one used for sign-up however it is my bank account that was linked and payment taken from.

I initially paid £1 and £0.95 for what we believed was a one-off online personality quiz. At no point during the payment process was it made clear that we were signing up for an ongoing subscription. Since then, two separate payments of £29.95 have been taken from my account, totalling £59.90, for a service we have not knowingly subscribed to or used.

The only reference to a subscription appears at the very bottom of a follow-up email, which is neither clear nor transparent. I do not believe this constitutes informed consent, and I feel the subscription terms were presented in a misleading way.

I am currently experiencing serious financial difficulty as a recently single mother to 3 children and discovering these charges has caused me significant distress. £59.90 is a substantial amount of money for me, particularly for a service I did not intend to purchase and have not used and I have been unable to buy groceries this week due to the recent payment being taken.

I am therefore requesting:

1. Immediate cancellation of the subscription, and
2. A full refund of the £59.90 taken from my account.

I hope this can be resolved promptly and amicably. Please confirm in writing that the subscription has been cancelled and that the refund is being processed.

I have attached photo of the payments taken from my account

I look forward to your response.

Thanks

Lauren Higham

Sonia Sivakova, Jan 14, 2026, 02:38

Hello,

I am writing regarding an unauthorized charge made by Personality.co.

I took a psychological test on your website and at no point did I knowingly or explicitly consent to a paid subscription. Nevertheless, I was subscribed to a paid plan and charged without my clear and informed approval.

According to the EU Consumer Rights Directive (2011/83/EU), a consumer must give explicit consent before any paid subscription is activated, and all pricing information must be clearly disclosed prior to payment. If such consent is not obtained, the charge is considered unauthorized.

Additionally, EU law grants consumers a 14-day right of withdrawal for digital services purchased online. I am exercising this right and requesting a full refund of the amount charged.

Please confirm:

- the immediate cancellation of the subscription (if not already completed),
- and the full refund of the charged amount to my original payment method.

If this issue is not resolved promptly, I will escalate the matter by filing a complaint with my bank and the relevant consumer protection authority.

I expect your confirmation within 5 business days.

Kind regards,

Sophija Sivakova

To the Customer Support Team at Personality.co,

I am writing to formally dispute a series of unauthorized charges made to my account starting in September 2025 and continuing to the present date in 2026.

I wish to state clearly that I only accessed your website to perform a personality test. At no point did I knowingly or explicitly consent to a recurring subscription or any additional paid services. Your platform uses deceptive marketing practices and "dark patterns" to hide subscription terms, which has led to these charges without my permission.

Based on these facts, I demand the following:

1. **Immediate Cancellation:** Cancel any active subscription or membership associated with my email address ([tu-correo@ejemplo.com]) and my payment method immediately.
2. **Full Refund:** I request a full refund of all monthly charges made since September 2025, as these services were never requested nor used.
3. **Data Deletion:** Delete all my personal and financial information from your database.

I am aware that many other users have reported similar deceptive billing experiences. If this matter is not resolved and the refunds are not processed within the next 5 business days, I will proceed to:

- File a formal dispute with my bank for "unauthorized/deceptive transactions."
- Report your company to the Better Business Bureau (BBB) and relevant consumer protection agencies.

I look forward to your immediate confirmation of the cancellation and the refund receipt.

Sincerely,



--

Patricia Torres - Calderón Cáceres

Pablo Palacio, Jan 14, 2026, 10:11

**To the Billing and Legal Department of Personality.Co,**

I am writing to formally demand the **immediate cancellation** of any subscription associated with my details and the **full refund** of two unauthorized charges made to my account:

-

**December 18, 2025:** €39.95

-

**January 13, 2026:** €39.95

-

**Total Amount Owed: €79.90**

I have never explicitly or knowingly consented to a monthly subscription. Your website uses **deceptive "dark patterns"**to hide recurring costs behind a supposedly free or low-cost personality test. This is a clear violation of European consumer protection laws regarding transparency and informed consent.

**Be advised of the following:**

1.

json.Title: This is a scam
json.Description:
----------------------------------------------

Laura Gieczewska, Jan 14, 2026, 13:06

I consider your tests to be deceptive and a form of subscription fraud.



There is no clear and explicit information stating that by completing the test and making what appears to be a one-time payment, the user is automatically enrolled in a recurring subscription. The way your website is deliberately designed makes this information unclear and misleading.



Please do not refer me to your terms and conditions. Hiding critical payment information in fine print or external regulations while presenting the service as a one-time purchase is a deceptive practice by design.



This constitutes a misleading commercial practice, and I therefore demand a full refund of the amount charged for completing the test.



Additionally, I inform you that your business practices have been reported to the appropriate European Union consumer protection authorities.



I expect confirmation of the refund without further delay.



Regards,



Laura Gieczewska

----------------------------------------------

zhangcenlu20000722, Jan 14, 2026, 16:27



To the Customer Service Team of Personality.co,


I am writing for the final time regarding your company's failure to fully refund two unauthorized, fraudulent charges.



To recap: on December 5, 2025 and January 2, 2026, your company charged my card £29.95 on each date without my knowledge or consent. Following my complaint, you issued a partial refund of only one transaction, leaving the £29.95 charge from December 5, 2025, still outstanding.



Nine full working days have now passed since your last acknowledgment, which far exceeds any reasonable or standard processing time for a refund. This delay is unacceptable and demonstrates a complete disregard for resolving your own error.



Key Facts & Demand:

Outstanding Unauthorized Transaction: December 5, 2025, for £29.95.

Time Elapsed: Over 9 working days since your commitment to resolve this.

Merchant Refund Processing: Typically completes within 3-10 business days.

My Demand: Immediate and unconditional refund of the remaining £29.95 to my original payment method.



Your act of issuing only a partial refund confirms the illegitimacy of these charges and your bad faith in resolving them. This is no longer merely an inconvenience; it is a deliberate withholding of funds.



This is a final notice: If I do not receive confirmation of the refund for the outstanding amount and see the funds returned within 48 hours of this email (by January 16, 2026), I will immediately proceed with all of the following escalation steps without further warning:

1. File a formal Chargeback (Dispute) with my card issuer for this specific transaction.

2. Submit detailed reports to the Financial Conduct Authority (FCA) and Action Fraud, highlighting your company's fraudulent charging and refusal to provide a full refund.

3. Publicly update my consumer reviews on all relevant platforms to warn others of your company's practice of unauthorized charging and incomplete refunds.



The choice is yours: correct this error immediately or face the formal consequences.



Sincerely,

Cenlu

Simran Dhonsi, Jan 15, 2026, 01:07

Dear Personality.co Support Team,

I am writing regarding an unauthorised charge of £29.95 taken from my account on 14 January 2026 following my purchase of an ADHD test for £1.95 on 7 January 2026.

Your website and checkout process clearly advertised “£1.95 to get your results.” At no point during my purchase was it made clear in a fair, prominent, or transparent way that I was entering into a recurring £29.95 subscription billed every four weeks.

This charge was therefore taken without my informed consent and is misleading and unlawful under UK consumer protection law, including:

- The Consumer Rights Act 2015
- The Consumer Contracts Regulations 2013
- The Consumer Protection from Unfair Trading Regulations 2008

I only discovered this subscription after the charge had already been taken, via an email that was sent to my spam folder - this does not constitute valid notice or consent.

I am therefore exercising my legal right to:

1. Cancel this subscription immediately

2. Receive a full refund of £29.95

3. Have confirmation that no further charges will be taken

If the refund is not issued within 7 days, I will:

- File a chargeback and fraud dispute with my bank
- Submit a formal complaint to UK Trading Standards and the Competition & Markets Authority (CMA)
- Report this to Action Fraud for deceptive and unfair commercial practices

Please treat this matter seriously. I expect written confirmation that the subscription has been cancelled and the refund processed.

Yours faithfully,

Simran Dhonsi

rory maher, Jan 15, 2026, 01:50

I’m writing regarding my recent charge under the email rory31@hotmail.com.

I kindly request for a refund of the £29.95 charge that happened on the 14th of January

I signed up for the £1.95 / 7-day access, believing it was a one-time fee. I was not clearly informed at the point of purchase that the plan would convert into a £29.95 recurring subscription, and only realised this after seeing the charge on my bank statement.

Outlining my consumer experience based on my booking flow:

The initial screen only advertised €1.95 for 7-day access (no subscription mention).

The recurring £29.95 charge was only shown below the “Get Instant Access” button, not before it — meaning it could easily be missed.

I received only one welcome email, and no clear reminder of the upcoming renewal.

Under EU consumer law (Consumer Rights Directive 2011/83/EU & Unfair Commercial Practices rules), subscription terms and auto-renewal must be clearly and prominently communicated before payment. As the recurring payment wasn’t sufficiently disclosed at the moment of purchase, I did not provide informed consent for ongoing billing.

I trust we can resolve it quickly and professionally.

Sent from Outlook for iOS

Antonina Didyk, Jan 15, 2026, 02:07

Hello,

I am writing regarding an unexpected charge from “personality.co” that just appeared on my bank statement.

**I did NOT knowingly or clearly consent to a recurring paid subscription.** I only used a short trial (“7-Day Full Access”) and was not adequately informed that it would automatically convert into a recurring charge of COP 123,329 every 4 weeks.

This charge was unexpected and unauthorized from my perspective.

I hereby request:

1.

Immediate cancellation of my subscription

2.

A full refund of the most recent charge

3.

Written confirmation that no further charges will be made

If this issue is not resolved promptly, I will initiate a formal dispute (chargeback) through my bank and card provider (Visa), citing insufficient disclosure and lack of informed consent.

Account details:

Name: Antonina Didyk

Email: antoninadidyk@gmail.com

I expect your response and resolution within 48 hours.

Regards,

Antonina Didyk
